title: relay: directory_list stamped sequence differs from sort sequence under concurrency
description: relay/src/ws.rs:442-446: `connection_seq()` is called twice per user — once for `sort_by_key` (line 442) and once for the stamp (line 446). Between these two calls a client may disconnect, causing `connection_seq()` to return `u64::MAX` for the stamp while the sort used the original sequence. Result: a DirectoryList with a user whose stamped `sequence` is `u64::MAX` but whose position in the array implies a lower sequence — clients building an admin election from this list would promote the wrong peer. Fix: compute `connection_seq()` once per user before sorting, store it in a local map, and reuse it for both sort and stamp.
